after.name: Tropical Liqueur
after.description: Sweet liqueur with exotic tropical fruit flavors. Ideal for tiki and summer cocktails.

before.name: Herbal Liqueur
before.description: Generic herbal liqueur.

after.name: Herbal Liqueur
after.description: Complex liqueur with a blend of various herbs and botanicals. Often used as a digestif or in classic cocktails.

before.name: Chartreuse Green
before.description: 110-proof French herbal liqueur made by Carthusian monks. 130 botanicals; iconic.

after.name: Chartreuse Green
after.description: High-proof French herbal liqueur made by Carthusian monks from 130 botanicals. An iconic and complex ingredient in many classic and modern cocktails.

before.name: Chartreuse Yellow
before.description: Sweeter, lower-proof yellow Chartreuse. Honey notes alongside the herbal core.

after.name: Chartreuse Yellow
after.description: Sweeter and lower-proof than Green Chartreuse, with prominent honey notes complementing its herbal core. Versatile in cocktails and as a digestif.

before.name: Benedictine
before.description: Herbal liqueur made by Benedictine monks since 1510. Vieux Carre and Singapore Sling.

after.name: Benedictine
after.description: French herbal liqueur produced by Benedictine monks since 1510. A key ingredient in classic cocktails like the Vieux Carré and Singapore Sling.

before.name: B and B
before.description: 50/50 blend of Benedictine and brandy. Pre-mixed for ease.

after.name: B&B
after.description: A pre-mixed blend of Benedictine herbal liqueur and French brandy. Offers a smoother, slightly drier profile than Benedictine alone, ideal for sipping.

before.name: Coffee Liqueur
before.description: Generic coffee liqueur. Espresso Martini base.

after.name: Coffee Liqueur
after.description: Sweet liqueur with a rich coffee flavor. A versatile base for Espresso Martinis and other dessert cocktails.

before.name: Kahlua
before.description: Coffee liqueur. White Russian and Espresso Martini standard.

after.name: Kahlua
after.description: Mexican coffee liqueur made with rum, arabica coffee, and vanilla. A standard ingredient for White Russians and Espresso Martinis.

before.name: Mr. Black Cold Brew Coffee Liqueur
before.description: Premium cold brew coffee liqueur. Less sweet, more coffee-forward than Kahlua.

after.name: Mr. Black Cold Brew Coffee Liqueur
after.description: Australian premium cold brew coffee liqueur. Known for its less sweet, more coffee-forward profile, making it excellent for sophisticated coffee cocktails.

before.name: Cream Liqueur
before.description: Generic cream liqueur.

after.name: Cream Liqueur
after.description: Sweet, creamy liqueur, often with a whiskey or neutral spirit base. Used in dessert cocktails and for sipping.

before.name: Bailey's Irish Cream
before.description: Irish whiskey cream liqueur. Mudslides and Mind Erasers.

after.name: Bailey's Irish Cream
after.description: Original Irish whiskey cream liqueur, blending Irish whiskey, cream, and cocoa. A popular ingredient in Mudslides and Mind Erasers.

before.name: Almond Liqueur
before.description: Generic almond liqueur (amaretto style).

after.name: Almond Liqueur
after.description: Sweet liqueur with a distinct almond flavor, often in the amaretto style. Essential for Amaretto Sours and other nutty cocktails.

before.name: Disaronno Originale
before.description: Almond-flavored liqueur (no actual almonds). Sour and Godfather standard.

after.name: Disaronno Originale
after.description: Iconic Italian amaretto-style liqueur with a distinctive almond flavor, though made from apricot kernel oil. A key ingredient in Amaretto Sours and Godfathers.

before.name: Hazelnut Liqueur
before.description: Generic hazelnut liqueur.

after.name: Hazelnut Liqueur
after.description: Sweet liqueur with a rich, toasted hazelnut flavor. Adds depth to dessert cocktails and coffee drinks.

before.name: Frangelico
before.description: Hazelnut liqueur. Used in Nutty Irishmans and dessert cocktails.

after.name: Frangelico
after.description: Italian hazelnut liqueur with notes of cocoa and vanilla. Popular in dessert cocktails like the Nutty Irishman and for sipping.

before.name: Vanilla Liqueur
before.description: Generic vanilla liqueur (Licor 43 / Tuaca style).

after.name: Vanilla Liqueur
after.description: Sweet liqueur with a prominent vanilla flavor. Versatile for adding warmth and sweetness to a wide range of cocktails.

before.name: Licor 43
before.description: Spanish 43-ingredient vanilla and citrus liqueur. Carajillo and modern dessert cocktails.

after.name: Licor 43
after.description: Spanish liqueur made from 43 botanicals, featuring prominent vanilla and citrus notes. Excellent in Carajillos and modern dessert cocktails.

before.name: Aperitif Bitter
before.description: Generic aperitif bitter (Campari / Aperol / Suze style).

after.name: Aperitif Bitter
after.description: A generic bitter aperitif liqueur, typically with a vibrant color and a balance of sweet and bitter notes. Ideal for spritzes and pre-dinner drinks.

before.name: Campari
before.description: Italian red aperitif bitter. The Negroni's defining ingredient.

after.name: Campari
after.description: Iconic Italian red aperitif bitter known for its distinct bittersweet flavor profile. The essential ingredient that defines the classic Negroni cocktail.

before.name: Aperol
before.description: Lighter, sweeter cousin of Campari. Foundation of the Aperol Spritz.

after.name: Aperol
after.description: Italian aperitif bitter, lighter and sweeter than Campari, with notes of orange and herbs. The foundation of the popular and refreshing Aperol Spritz.

before.name: Cynar
before.description: Artichoke-based amaro. Vegetal bitterness; great in spritzes and stirred.

after.name: Cynar
after.description: Artichoke-based Italian amaro with a distinctive vegetal bitterness. Excellent in spritzes, stirred cocktails, and as a digestif.

before.name: Brandy
before.description: Generic grape brandy. Use for cocktails that don't require Cognac's region specificity.

after.name: Brandy
after.description: Generic grape brandy, distilled from wine. Suitable for cocktails that do not require the specific regional characteristics of Cognac or Armagnac.

before.name: Laird's Bonded Apple Brandy
before.description: 100-proof bonded apple brandy. Used in Jack Roses and Pink Ladies.

after.name: Laird's Bonded Apple Brandy
after.description: 100-proof bonded apple brandy, aged for at least four years. A robust and fruity spirit, essential for classic cocktails like the Jack Rose and Pink Lady.

before.name: Amaro
before.description: Generic amaro for any-digestive recipes.

after.name: Amaro
after.description: Generic Italian herbal liqueur, typically bittersweet and consumed as a digestif. Versatile for adding complexity to cocktails.

before.name: Fernet-Branca
before.description: Intensely bitter Italian amaro. The bartender's handshake.

after.name: Fernet-Branca
after.description: Intensely bitter Italian amaro with notes of mint and eucalyptus. A classic digestif and bartender's handshake.

before.name: Averna
before.description: Sweet, gentle Sicilian amaro. Approachable entry point to amari.

after.name: Averna
after.description: Sweet, gentle Sicilian amaro with caramel and orange notes. An approachable entry point to the amaro category.

before.name: Montenegro
before.description: Floral Italian amaro. Approachable; rose and orange notes.

after.name: Montenegro
after.description: Floral Italian amaro with rose and orange notes, balanced by a mild bitterness. Versatile in cocktails and as a digestif.

before.name: Nonino Quintessentia
before.description: Grappa-based amaro. Premium; central to the Paper Plane.

after.name: Nonino Quintessentia
after.description: Grappa-based Italian amaro with notes of orange, caramel, and bitter herbs. A premium spirit, central to the Paper Plane cocktail.

before.name: Other Liqueur
before.description: Catchall liqueur.

after.name: Other Liqueur
after.description: A general-purpose liqueur for various cocktail applications.

before.name: Pimm's No. 1
before.description: Gin-based fruit cup liqueur. Foundation of the Pimm's Cup.

after.name: Pimm's No. 1
after.description: Gin-based fruit cup liqueur with notes of citrus, spice, and botanicals. The essential foundation for the refreshing Pimm's Cup.

before.name: Bianco Vermouth
before.description: Generic bianco vermouth.

after.name: Bianco Vermouth
after.description: Generic sweet white vermouth, often with floral and citrus notes. Used in modern white Negronis and spritzes.

before.name: Cinzano Bianco
before.description: Sweet white vermouth. Used in modern white Negronis and spritzes.

after.name: Cinzano Bianco
after.description: Sweet white vermouth from Italy, offering floral and citrus notes. Excellent in modern white Negronis and spritzes.

before.name: Aperitif Wine
before.description: Generic aperitif wine (Lillet / Cocchi style).

after.name: Aperitif Wine
after.description: Generic fortified aperitif wine, typically with bitter and citrus characteristics. Suitable for spritzes and light cocktails.

before.name: Lillet Blanc
before.description: Fortified aperitif wine. Used in Vesper martini and modern brunch cocktails.

after.name: Lillet Blanc
after.description: Fortified aperitif wine from Bordeaux, France, with notes of candied orange, honey, and pine. Used in the Vesper martini and spritzes.

before.name: Lillet Rosé
before.description: Pink Lillet introduced 2012. Strawberry notes alongside the classic profile.

after.name: Lillet Rosé
after.description: Pink fortified aperitif wine from Bordeaux, France, with notes of red berries, grapefruit, and orange blossom. Great in spritzes.

before.name: Cocchi Americano
before.description: Quinine-fortified aperitif wine. Substitute for original Kina Lillet.

after.name: Cocchi Americano
after.description: Quinine-fortified aperitif wine from Piedmont, Italy, with bitter orange and gentian notes. A classic substitute for original Kina Lillet.

before.name: Bonal Gentiane Quina
before.description: Bitter-sweet aperitif wine with gentian and cinchona. Cult bartender ingredient.

after.name: Bonal Gentiane Quina
after.description: Bitter-sweet aperitif wine from France, featuring gentian and cinchona. A cult bartender ingredient for complex cocktails.

before.name: Amber
before.description: Generic amber ale or lager.

after.name: Amber
after.description: Generic amber ale or lager, typically offering a balance of caramel malt sweetness and moderate hop bitterness.

before.name: Amber Ale
before.description: Generic amber ale. Caramel malt with moderate hop balance.

after.name: Amber Ale
after.description: Generic amber ale, characterized by caramel malt flavors and a balanced hop profile. A versatile, easy-drinking beer style.

before.name: Cider
before.description: Generic hard cider.

after.name: Cider
after.description: Generic hard cider, made from fermented apple juice. Offers a crisp, fruity, and often sweet profile.

before.name: Hard Seltzer
before.description: Generic hard seltzer.

after.name: Hard Seltzer
after.description: Generic hard seltzer, a light and effervescent alcoholic beverage. Often flavored with fruit essences.

before.name: Flavored Vodka
before.description: Generic flavored vodka. Catchall for citrus, vanilla, berry, and other infused vodka SKUs.

after.name: Flavored Vodka
after.description: Generic flavored vodka, encompassing various infusions like citrus, vanilla, or berry. Versatile for adding specific notes to cocktails.

before.name: Plymouth Gin
before.description: Generic Plymouth-style gin. Earthier and slightly sweeter than London Dry.

after.name: Plymouth Gin
after.description: Generic Plymouth-style gin, known for its earthier and slightly sweeter profile compared to London Dry. A classic gin for cocktails.

before.name: Plymouth
before.description: Geographically protected gin from Plymouth. Earthier and slightly sweeter than London Dry.

after.name: Plymouth Gin
after.description: Geographically protected gin from Plymouth, England. Earthier and slightly sweeter than London Dry, with prominent juniper and citrus.

before.name: Sweet Vermouth
before.description: Generic sweet (rosso) vermouth. Italian-style red vermouth used in Manhattan, Negroni, Boulevardier, and stirred whiskey cocktails.

after.name: Sweet Vermouth
after.description: Generic sweet (rosso) vermouth, an Italian-style red vermouth. Essential for Manhattans, Negronis, and other stirred whiskey cocktails.

before.name: Dolin Rouge
before.description: Lighter, more delicate sweet vermouth. Modern Manhattan favorite.

after.name: Dolin Rouge
after.description: Lighter, more delicate sweet vermouth from Savoie, France, with notes of fruit, spice, and alpine herbs. A modern Manhattan favorite.

before.name: Carpano Antica Formula
before.description: Premium sweet vermouth. Vanilla and chocolate richness; cocktail bar standard.

after.name: Carpano Antica Formula
after.description: Premium sweet vermouth from Italy, renowned for its rich vanilla, dark chocolate, and dried fruit notes. A cocktail bar standard.

before.name: Cinzano Rosso
before.description: Affordable Italian sweet vermouth. Common well red vermouth.

after.name: Cinzano Rosso
after.description: Affordable Italian sweet vermouth from Piedmont, offering a balanced profile of herbs, spices, and caramel. A common well red vermouth.

before.name: Cocchi Vermouth di Torino
before.description: Premium Torino-style sweet vermouth. Bitter chocolate and herbal notes.

after.name: Cocchi Vermouth di Torino
after.description: Premium Torino-style sweet vermouth from Piedmont, Italy, featuring bitter chocolate, orange, and herbal notes. Excellent in Negronis.

before.name: Dry Vermouth
before.description: Generic dry (French) vermouth. Bartender default for martinis and any drink calling for "dry vermouth" without naming a brand.

after.name: Dry Vermouth
after.description: Generic dry (French) vermouth. A bartender's default for martinis and other cocktails calling for dry vermouth without naming a specific brand.

before.name: Dolin Dry Vermouth
before.description: French dry vermouth. Bartender default for martinis.

after.name: Dolin Dry
after.description: A classic French dry vermouth from Chambéry. Known for its delicate, floral, and alpine herbal character, making it a preferred choice for martinis.

before.name: Martini and Rossi Extra Dry
before.description: Mass-market dry vermouth. Common well dry vermouth.

after.name: Martini & Rossi Extra Dry
after.description: A widely available Italian dry vermouth, known for its crisp and slightly bitter profile. Often used as a well vermouth in classic cocktails like the Martini.

before.name: Rhum Agricole
before.description: Generic rhum agricole. Fresh sugarcane juice base; grassy and earthy.

after.name: Rhum Agricole
after.description: A style of rum made directly from fresh sugarcane juice, rather than molasses. It offers a distinctive grassy, vegetal, and earthy character, often used in Ti' Punch.

before.name: Rhum Clement VSOP
before.description: Aged rhum agricole made from sugarcane juice. Grass and earth instead of molasses.

after.name: Clément VSOP
after.description: An aged Rhum Agricole from Martinique, distilled from fresh sugarcane juice and aged in oak barrels. It presents notes of grass, spice, and tropical fruit, ideal for sipping or in cocktails.

before.name: Gin
before.description: Generic gin reference. Default to a London Dry style unless recipe calls for specific category.

after.name: Gin
after.description: A generic reference for gin, typically implying a London Dry style. Characterized by a dominant juniper flavor, it is a versatile base for numerous classic cocktails.

before.name: London Dry Gin
before.description: Classic dry gin style. Juniper-forward with citrus and coriander.

after.name: London Dry Gin
after.description: A classic, juniper-forward gin style with prominent citrus and coriander notes. It forms the backbone of many iconic cocktails like the Gin & Tonic and Martini.

before.name: Tanqueray London Dry
before.description: Classic London Dry gin. Juniper-forward with coriander and angelica. The default cocktail gin for many bartenders.

after.name: Tanqueray London Dry
after.description: A quintessential London Dry gin, renowned for its bold juniper character balanced with notes of coriander, angelica, and licorice. It is a versatile and reliable choice for a wide range of gin cocktails.

before.name: Tanqueray No. Ten
before.description: Tanqueray's premium expression. Fresh citrus and chamomile from a small still ("Tiny Ten").

after.name: Tanqueray No. Ten
after.description: Tanqueray's premium gin, distilled in small batches with fresh citrus fruits and chamomile flowers. It offers a bright, vibrant, and exceptionally smooth profile, perfect for elevated gin cocktails.

before.name: Bombay Sapphire
before.description: Vapor-infused London Dry gin. Lighter juniper than Tanqueray with prominent botanical character.

after.name: Bombay Sapphire
after.description: A vapor-infused London Dry gin known for its lighter juniper profile and prominent botanical character, including notes of citrus, almond, and cassia bark. It creates a smooth and aromatic base for cocktails.

before.name: Overproof Rum
before.description: Generic overproof rum. 114+ proof; tiki backbone.

after.name: Overproof Rum
after.description: A high-proof rum, typically 114 proof or higher, providing intense flavor and alcoholic kick. Essential for many tiki cocktails and for adding depth to rum-based drinks.

before.name: Smith and Cross
before.description: Navy strength Jamaican pot still rum. Intense funk; backbone of many tiki recipes.

after.name: Smith & Cross Traditional Jamaica Rum
after.description: A navy strength Jamaican pot still rum, famous for its intense 'funk' and high ester content. It is a crucial ingredient for adding depth and complexity to classic tiki cocktails.

before.name: Wray and Nephew White Overproof
before.description: White overproof Jamaican rum. Classic Tiki and Caribbean cocktail booster.

after.name: Wray & Nephew White Overproof
after.description: A powerful, unaged white overproof Jamaican rum, known for its bold and fruity 'funk' and high alcohol content. It is a staple in Caribbean cocktails and a popular choice for adding a kick to drinks.

before.name: Hendrick's
before.description: Scottish gin with cucumber and rose. Distinctive perfumed style; serves traditionally with cucumber.

after.name: Hendrick's Gin
after.description: A distinctive Scottish gin infused with rose and cucumber, offering a unique perfumed and refreshing character. Traditionally served with cucumber, it is a popular choice for G&Ts and other modern gin cocktails.

before.name: The Botanist
before.description: Made by Bruichladdich on Islay. 22 hand-foraged Islay botanicals.

after.name: The Botanist Islay Dry Gin
after.description: An artisanal gin from Islay, made with 22 hand-foraged local botanicals. It offers a complex and herbaceous profile with notes of mint, citrus, and floral elements, perfect for sophisticated gin cocktails.

before.name: Aviation
before.description: New American gin style. Less juniper-heavy, with lavender and cardamom.

after.name: Aviation American Gin
after.description: A New American style gin with a less juniper-dominant profile, featuring prominent notes of lavender, cardamom, and sarsaparilla. It offers a smooth, floral, and subtly spiced character, ideal for modern gin cocktails.

before.name: Monkey 47
before.description: Black Forest gin made with 47 botanicals. Complex and pricey.

after.name: Monkey 47 Schwarzwald Dry Gin
after.description: A highly complex and acclaimed German gin, crafted with 47 hand-picked botanicals from the Black Forest. It delivers a rich tapestry of floral, fruity, and spicy notes, making it a luxurious choice for gin enthusiasts.

before.name: Empress 1908
before.description: Indigo color from butterfly pea flower; turns pink with citrus. Visual impact gin.

after.name: Empress 1908 Indigo Gin
after.description: A visually striking gin that derives its indigo color from butterfly pea flower, which changes to pink with the addition of citrus. It offers a soft, floral, and citrusy profile, perfect for visually appealing cocktails.

before.name: White Rum
before.description: Light, clear rum. Used in daiquiris, mojitos, and Cuban cocktails.

after.name: White Rum
after.description: A light, clear, and typically unaged rum, known for its clean and subtle flavor profile. It is a foundational spirit for classic cocktails like the Daiquiri and Mojito.

before.name: Bacardi Superior
before.description: Light, filtered Cuban-style rum. The default white rum for daiquiris and mojitos in most bars.

after.name: Bacardi Superior
after.description: A classic light-bodied, charcoal-filtered white rum with a clean and subtle flavor profile. It is the quintessential choice for crafting iconic Cuban cocktails such as the Daiquiri and Mojito.

before.name: Don Q Cristal
before.description: Light Puerto Rican rum. Preferred at many cocktail bars over Bacardi for daiquiris.

after.name: Don Q Cristal
after.description: A clean and crisp Puerto Rican white rum, charcoal-filtered for purity and smoothness. It offers a delicate balance of sweet and subtle notes, making it a preferred base for refreshing daiquiris and other rum cocktails.

before.name: Plantation 3 Stars
before.description: Blend of Trinidad, Barbados, and Jamaica rums. Modern bar workhorse white rum.

after.name: Plantation 3 Stars White Rum
after.description: A versatile white rum blend sourced from Trinidad, Barbados, and Jamaica, offering a complex profile with notes of tropical fruit, cane sugar, and a hint of funk. It serves as a modern bar workhorse for a variety of rum cocktails.

before.name: Mount Gay Eclipse
before.description: Bajan blended rum. Classic for rum punches and Hurricanes.

after.name: Mount Gay Eclipse Rum
after.description: A classic Barbadian blended rum, lightly aged in oak barrels to impart a golden hue and mellow flavor. It offers notes of vanilla, banana, and almond, making it ideal for rum punches and tropical cocktails.

before.name: Gold Rum
before.description: Generic gold (amber) rum. Lightly aged blend; rum punches and Hurricanes.

after.name: Gold Rum
after.description: A lightly aged rum, typically gaining its golden color and mellow flavor from brief barrel maturation. It is a versatile spirit often used in rum punches, Hurricanes, and other tropical drinks.

before.name: Aged Rum
before.description: Rum aged in oak. Color and depth from the barrel; used in Mai Tais and Old Fashioneds.

after.name: Aged Rum
after.description: Rum matured in oak barrels, developing a richer color and more complex flavor profile with notes of vanilla, caramel, and spice. It is excellent for sipping or in stirred cocktails like the Mai Tai and Old Fashioned.

before.name: Bacardi 8
before.description: Aged 8 years. Used as a sipping rum and in stirred rum cocktails.

after.name: Bacardi Ocho
after.description: Aged 8 years, this rum is smooth and balanced. Ideal for sipping or in classic stirred rum cocktails.

before.name: Mount Gay Black Barrel
before.description: Mount Gay finished in heavily charred bourbon barrels.

after.name: Mount Gay Black Barrel
after.description: Barbadian rum finished in heavily charred bourbon barrels. Offers a rich, spicy character, great for Old Fashioneds.

before.name: Appleton Estate Reserve Blend
before.description: Jamaican blended rum with funky pot still character. Used in Mai Tais and tiki drinks.

after.name: Appleton Estate Reserve Blend
after.description: Jamaican blended rum with a distinctive funky pot still character. A versatile choice for Mai Tais and other tiki drinks.

before.name: Diplomatico Reserva Exclusiva
before.description: Sweet, dessert-style aged rum. Popular for sipping and rum Old Fashioneds.

after.name: Diplomatico Reserva Exclusiva
after.description: A sweet, dessert-style aged rum from Venezuela. Popular for sipping neat or in rum Old Fashioneds.

before.name: Dark Rum
before.description: Heavily caramel-colored, often molasses-rich rum. Tiki staple; used in Dark and Stormy.

after.name: Dark Rum
after.description: Heavily caramel-colored, molasses-rich rum. A tiki staple, essential for a Dark and Stormy.

before.name: Goslings Black Seal
before.description: Bermudian dark rum. Required for an authentic Dark and Stormy.

after.name: Goslings Black Seal
after.description: Bermudian dark rum, known for its rich, complex flavor. The required spirit for an authentic Dark and Stormy.

before.name: Spiced Rum
before.description: Rum infused with spices like vanilla and cinnamon. Sweeter highball style.

after.name: Spiced Rum
after.description: Rum infused with spices like vanilla and cinnamon. A sweeter, approachable spirit often used in highballs.

before.name: Captain Morgan Original Spiced
before.description: Best-selling spiced rum in the US. Vanilla-forward; mass market.

after.name: Captain Morgan Original Spiced
after.description: The best-selling spiced rum, known for its vanilla-forward, mass-market appeal. Great for simple mixed drinks.

before.name: Sailor Jerry Spiced
before.description: Higher-proof spiced rum than Captain Morgan. Vanilla and cinnamon dominant.

after.name: Sailor Jerry Spiced
after.description: A higher-proof spiced rum with dominant vanilla and cinnamon notes. Offers a bolder flavor for spiced rum cocktails.

before.name: Blanco Tequila
before.description: Unaged tequila. Sharp agave character; default for margaritas and palomas.

after.name: Blanco Tequila
after.description: Unaged tequila with a sharp, vibrant agave character. The default choice for margaritas and palomas.

before.name: Patron Silver
before.description: Premium blanco tequila. Smooth, slightly sweet; mass-market premium.

after.name: Patron Silver
after.description: Premium blanco tequila, known for its smooth, slightly sweet profile. A popular choice for upscale tequila cocktails.

before.name: Don Julio Blanco
before.description: Premium blanco. Bright agave with white pepper finish.

after.name: Don Julio Blanco
after.description: A premium blanco tequila offering bright agave notes with a crisp white pepper finish. Excellent for fresh, clean cocktails.

before.name: Casamigos Blanco
before.description: Celebrity-founded blanco. Smooth and sweet; popular at upscale cocktail bars.

after.name: Casamigos Blanco
after.description: A celebrity-founded blanco tequila, celebrated for its smooth and sweet profile. Popular in upscale cocktail bars.

before.name: Herradura Silver
before.description: Silver tequila aged 45 days (legally still blanco). Earthier than most blancos.

after.name: Herradura Silver
after.description: Silver tequila aged 45 days, offering an earthier character than most blancos. Adds depth to tequila-based drinks.

before.name: The Macallan 12 Double Cask
before.description: Sherry and bourbon cask blend. Premium Speyside single malt.

after.name: Macallan Double Cask
after.description: A premium Speyside single malt Scotch, matured in both sherry and bourbon casks. Offers a rich, balanced flavor for sipping.

before.name: Scotch Whisky
before.description: Generic Scotch reference. Default to a blended style; single malts vary widely.

after.name: Scotch Whisky
after.description: Generic blended Scotch whisky, offering a versatile base. Suitable for highballs or simple Scotch cocktails.

before.name: Johnnie Walker Black Label
before.description: Blended Scotch aged at least 12 years. Common premium pour and Penicillin base.

after.name: Johnnie Walker Black Label
after.description: A blended Scotch aged at least 12 years, known for its smoky, rich character. A common premium pour and base for a Penicillin.

before.name: Johnnie Walker Red Label
before.description: Entry-level Johnnie Walker blended Scotch. Mass market.

after.name: Johnnie Walker Red Label
after.description: An entry-level blended Scotch, offering a vibrant, smoky-sweet profile. A mass-market choice for mixed drinks.

before.name: Dewar's White Label
before.description: Approachable blended Scotch. Common well Scotch.

after.name: Dewar's White Label
after.description: An approachable blended Scotch, known for its smooth, honeyed character. A common well Scotch for everyday cocktails.

before.name: Reposado Tequila
before.description: Tequila aged 2 to 12 months in oak. Softer and rounder than blanco.

after.name: Reposado Tequila
after.description: Tequila aged 2 to 12 months in oak, resulting in a softer, rounder profile than blanco. Great for sipping or nuanced cocktails.

before.name: Casamigos Reposado
before.description: Reposado from Casamigos. Caramel and oak with the softness Casamigos is known for.

after.name: Casamigos Reposado
after.description: A reposado tequila from Casamigos, featuring caramel and oak notes with its signature smoothness. Ideal for sipping or elevated cocktails.

before.name: Herradura Reposado
before.description: Reposado aged 11 months. Long-aged for a reposado, with deep oak character.

after.name: Herradura Reposado
after.description: A reposado aged 11 months, offering a deep oak character due to its longer aging. Adds complexity to tequila drinks.

before.name: Cazadores Reposado
before.description: Mid-tier reposado. Common well tequila in cocktail-forward bars.

after.name: Cazadores Reposado
after.description: A mid-tier reposado tequila, known for its balanced agave and oak notes. A common well tequila in cocktail-forward bars.

before.name: Anejo Tequila
before.description: Tequila aged 1 to 3 years. Whiskey-like depth; used in stirred tequila drinks.

after.name: Anejo Tequila
after.description: Tequila aged 1 to 3 years, developing a whiskey-like depth and complexity. Best for sipping or stirred tequila drinks.

before.name: Don Julio 1942
before.description: Don Julio's premium anejo. Distinctive bottle; popular for sipping.

after.name: Don Julio 1942
after.description: Don Julio's premium anejo tequila, known for its distinctive bottle and smooth, rich flavor. Highly popular for sipping.

before.name: Mezcal
before.description: Smoky agave spirit. Generally roasted in earthen pits before distillation.

after.name: Mezcal
after.description: Smoky agave spirit, generally roasted in earthen pits before distillation. It offers a complex profile, often used in cocktails like the Mezcal Negroni or Paloma.

before.name: Del Maguey Vida
before.description: Single-village espadin mezcal. The cocktail mezcal default; affordable enough for mixing.

after.name: Vida
after.description: Single-village espadin mezcal, known for its balance of smoke and fruit. It is the cocktail mezcal default, affordable enough for mixing in drinks like the Mezcal Margarita.

before.name: Ilegal Joven
before.description: Smooth, lighter mezcal style. Popular in upscale cocktail programs.

after.name: Joven
after.description: Smooth, unaged espadin mezcal with a lighter smoke profile. It is versatile for cocktails and often enjoyed neat, popular in upscale bar programs.

before.name: Montelobos
before.description: Joven espadin mezcal. Vibrant smoke and citrus.

after.name: Montelobos
after.description: Organic espadin mezcal known for its vibrant smoke, citrus, and herbal notes. It is a well-regarded choice for both sipping and mixing in agave-based cocktails.

before.name: Whiskey
before.description: Generic whiskey reference. Use for recipes (like Old Fashioned) that work across whiskey styles.

after.name: Whiskey
after.description: Generic whiskey for recipes that work across various styles. It provides a versatile base for classic stirred cocktails like the Old Fashioned and Whiskey Sour.

before.name: Bourbon
before.description: Generic bourbon reference. Sweeter, corn-forward whiskey. Default to roughly 90 proof brand.

after.name: Bourbon
after.description: Generic bourbon, a sweeter and corn-forward American whiskey. It is a versatile base for many cocktails, including the Mint Julep and Whiskey Sour.

before.name: Maker's Mark
before.description: Wheated bourbon, no rye in mash bill. Soft, sweet character with red wax dipped bottle.

after.name: Maker's Mark
after.description: Wheated Kentucky bourbon, soft and sweet from no rye in the mash bill. Used in stirred whiskey cocktails and Old Fashioneds.

before.name: Buffalo Trace
before.description: Standard rye-recipe bourbon from Buffalo Trace Distillery. Award winner; widely used in cocktails.

after.name: Buffalo Trace
after.description: Standard rye-recipe bourbon from Buffalo Trace Distillery. Award winner and widely used in classic bourbon cocktails.

before.name: Woodford Reserve
before.description: Premium small-batch bourbon. Triple-distilled (unusual for bourbon); fruit-forward.

after.name: Woodford Reserve
after.description: Premium small-batch bourbon, triple-distilled for a fruit-forward character. Excellent for sipping or in elevated whiskey cocktails.

before.name: Bulleit Bourbon
before.description: High-rye bourbon (~28% rye in mash). Spicier than typical bourbons; cocktail-friendly.

after.name: Bulleit Bourbon
after.description: High-rye bourbon with a spicier profile than typical bourbons. It is a versatile and cocktail-friendly choice for many whiskey drinks.

before.name: Rye Whiskey
before.description: Generic rye reference. Spicier and drier than bourbon. Default for Manhattans, Sazeracs.

after.name: Rye Whiskey
after.description: Generic rye whiskey, spicier and drier than bourbon. It is the default spirit for classic cocktails like the Manhattan and Sazerac.

before.name: Bulleit Rye
before.description: High-rye mash (95% rye, 5% malted barley). Wide bar distribution.

after.name: Bulleit Rye
after.description: High-rye mash (95% rye, 5% malted barley) offering a bold, spicy character. It is widely distributed and a popular choice for rye cocktails.

before.name: Rittenhouse Rye Bottled in Bond
before.description: 100-proof bonded rye. The cocktail bartender's default rye for Manhattans and Sazeracs.

after.name: Rittenhouse Rye
after.description: 100-proof bonded rye whiskey, known for its robust and spicy profile. It is a bartender's default for classic rye cocktails like Manhattans and Sazeracs.

before.name: Sazerac Rye
before.description: Buffalo Trace's entry rye. The eponymous spirit for the Sazerac cocktail.

after.name: Sazerac Rye
after.description: Buffalo Trace's entry rye, the eponymous spirit for the classic Sazerac cocktail. It offers a balanced profile of spice and sweetness.

before.name: High West Double Rye
before.description: Blend of two rye whiskeys. Modern American craft rye.

after.name: Double Rye
after.description: A blend of two rye whiskeys, known for its bold and complex modern American craft rye character. It is excellent in Manhattans or neat.

before.name: Navy Strength Gin
before.description: Generic 114-proof gin. Higher proof carries botanicals through citrus and tonic.

after.name: Navy Strength Gin
after.description: Generic 114-proof gin, with a higher proof that allows botanicals to shine through in cocktails. Ideal for Gin & Tonics or powerful gin-based drinks.

before.name: Genever
before.description: Generic Dutch genever. Malt-base gin ancestor.

after.name: Genever
after.description: Generic Dutch genever, the malt-base ancestor of gin. It offers a rich, malty character distinct from modern gins, suitable for classic cocktails.

before.name: Old Tom Gin
before.description: Generic Old Tom gin. Sweetened pre-Prohibition style.

after.name: Old Tom Gin
after.description: Generic Old Tom gin, a slightly sweetened pre-Prohibition style. It bridges the gap between genever and London Dry, ideal for Tom Collins and Martinez cocktails.

before.name: Cointreau
before.description: Premium triple sec. The default orange liqueur in Margaritas and Sidecars.

after.name: Cointreau
after.description: Premium triple sec, known for its balanced sweet and bitter orange notes. It is the default orange liqueur in classic cocktails like Margaritas and Sidecars.

before.name: Triple Sec
before.description: Generic orange liqueur. Lower-quality alternative to Cointreau.

after.name: Triple Sec
after.description: Generic orange liqueur, offering a sweet and citrusy profile. It is a common ingredient in many fruit-forward and tropical cocktails.

before.name: Grand Marnier
before.description: Cognac-based orange liqueur. Richer and rounder than Cointreau; used in B-52 and Cadillac Margarita.

after.name: Grand Marnier
after.description: Cognac-based orange liqueur, offering a richer and rounder profile than Cointreau. It is used in cocktails like the B-52 and Cadillac Margarita.

before.name: Pierre Ferrand Dry Curacao
before.description: Cognac-based orange liqueur designed for classic cocktails. Bartender favorite.

after.name: Dry Curaçao
after.description: Cognac-based orange liqueur, crafted for classic cocktails with a drier, more complex orange flavor. It is a bartender favorite for its historical accuracy.

before.name: Combier Liqueur d'Orange
before.description: Original triple sec recipe from 1834. Cocktail-quality orange liqueur.

after.name: Liqueur d'Orange
after.description: The original triple sec recipe from 1834, offering a balanced and complex orange flavor. It is a cocktail-quality orange liqueur for discerning palates.

before.name: Irish Whiskey
before.description: Generic Irish whiskey. Smoother and lighter than Scotch; triple distillation typical.

after.name: Irish Whiskey
after.description: Generic Irish whiskey, typically smoother and lighter than Scotch due to triple distillation. It is a versatile spirit for sipping or in Irish Coffee and other cocktails.

before.name: Jameson
before.description: Triple-distilled Irish blended whiskey. Best-selling Irish whiskey worldwide.

after.name: Jameson
after.description: Triple-distilled Irish blended whiskey, known for its smooth and approachable character. It is the best-selling Irish whiskey worldwide, popular neat or in cocktails.

before.name: Tullamore D.E.W.
before.description: Triple-distilled triple-blend Irish whiskey. Smooth and approachable.

after.name: Tullamore D.E.W.
after.description: Triple-distilled, triple-blend Irish whiskey, known for its smooth and approachable character. A versatile spirit for sipping or mixing in classic cocktails.

before.name: Bushmills Original
before.description: Northern Irish blended whiskey. Lighter and slightly fruitier than Jameson.

after.name: Bushmills Original
after.description: A blend of triple-distilled malt whiskey and a lighter grain whiskey. Known for its approachable, slightly fruity, and honeyed profile, suitable for various cocktails.

before.name: Redbreast 12
before.description: Single pot still Irish whiskey. Premium category benchmark.

after.name: Redbreast 12
after.description: A benchmark single pot still Irish whiskey, aged for 12 years in a combination of bourbon and sherry casks. Rich, spicy, and complex, ideal for sipping neat or in premium cocktails.

before.name: St-Germain
before.description: Elderflower liqueur. The "bartender's ketchup" of the 2010s; ubiquitous in modern cocktails.

after.name: St-Germain
after.description: An artisanal French elderflower liqueur, known for its delicate floral and fruity notes. A versatile and popular ingredient in modern cocktails, adding brightness and complexity.

before.name: Crème de Violette
before.description: Violet flower liqueur. Aviation cocktail color and flavor.

after.name: Crème de Violette
after.description: A vibrant purple liqueur made from violet flowers, imparting a distinct floral aroma and flavor. Essential for classic cocktails like the Aviation, providing both color and taste.

before.name: Chambord
before.description: Black raspberry liqueur. French Martini and Kir Royale enhancer.

after.name: Chambord
after.description: A luxurious French black raspberry liqueur, blended with blackberries, honey, vanilla, and Cognac. Adds rich berry flavor and a beautiful color to cocktails like the French Martini and Kir Royale.

before.name: Crème de Cassis
before.description: Blackcurrant liqueur. Kir and Kir Royale.

after.name: Crème de Cassis
after.description: A sweet, dark red liqueur made from blackcurrants, originating from Burgundy, France. Essential for classic cocktails like the Kir and Kir Royale, adding a tart and fruity depth.

before.name: Sloe Gin
before.description: Gin infused with sloe berries. Sloe Gin Fizz; sweet-tart character.

after.name: Sloe Gin
after.description: A traditional British liqueur made by infusing sloe berries (wild plums) in gin, resulting in a sweet-tart, ruby-red spirit. A key ingredient in the Sloe Gin Fizz and other refreshing cocktails.

before.name: Limoncello
before.description: Lemon zest liqueur from southern Italy. Served chilled neat or in spritzes.

after.name: Limoncello
after.description: A traditional Italian lemon liqueur, made from the zest of Sorrento lemons steeped in alcohol. Known for its bright, sweet, and intensely lemony flavor, typically served chilled as a digestivo or in spritzes.

before.name: Cognac
before.description: Brandy from the Cognac region of France. Used in Sidecars, Sazeracs, Vieux Carre.

after.name: Cognac
after.description: A prestigious type of brandy produced in the Cognac region of France, distilled from grapes and aged in oak barrels. Offers a complex profile of fruit, floral, and oak notes, used in classic cocktails like the Sidecar and Sazerac.

before.name: Hennessy VS
before.description: Most-sold Cognac globally. Entry-level VS aged at least 2 years.

after.name: Hennessy VS
after.description: Hennessy's Very Special (VS) Cognac, aged for at least two years in French oak barrels. An accessible and versatile expression with vibrant fruit and oak notes, suitable for mixing in a wide range of cocktails.

before.name: Hennessy VSOP
before.description: Mid-tier Cognac aged at least 4 years. Used in Sidecars and stirred Cognac drinks.

after.name: Hennessy VSOP
after.description: Hennessy's Very Superior Old Pale (VSOP) Cognac, aged for at least four years. Offers a more refined and complex profile than VS, with notes of fruit, spice, and subtle oak, excellent for stirred cocktails or sipping.

before.name: Remy Martin VSOP
before.description: VSOP from a top Cognac house. Bright fruit and oak.

after.name: Remy Martin VSOP
after.description: A Very Superior Old Pale (VSOP) Cognac from Remy Martin, known for its bright fruit and oak characteristics. Aged for a minimum of four years, it's a versatile spirit for both sipping and crafting sophisticated cocktails.

before.name: Pierre Ferrand 1840 Original Formula
before.description: Specifically made for cocktails; replicates 19th-century Cognac style. Bartender favorite.

after.name: Pierre Ferrand 1840 Original Formula
after.description: A Cognac specifically designed for cocktails, replicating the higher proof and richer style of 19th-century Cognacs. Its robust character stands up well in classic and modern mixed drinks, making it a bartender favorite.

before.name: Crème de Menthe (Green)
before.description: Mint-flavored green liqueur. Grasshopper and Stinger.

after.name: Crème de Menthe Green
after.description: A sweet, mint-flavored green liqueur, often used to impart a refreshing minty taste and color to dessert cocktails. Essential for classics like the Grasshopper and Stinger.

before.name: Crème de Cacao (White)
before.description: Clear chocolate liqueur. Brandy Alexander and dessert cocktails.

after.name: Crème de Cacao White
after.description: A clear, sweet liqueur with a distinct chocolate flavor, made from cacao beans. A key ingredient in dessert cocktails like the Brandy Alexander, adding rich cacao notes without altering color.

before.name: Crème de Cacao (Dark)
before.description: Dark chocolate liqueur. Color and depth in dessert and martini-style drinks.

after.name: Crème de Cacao Dark
after.description: A sweet, dark brown liqueur with a rich chocolate flavor, made from cacao beans. Used to add depth, color, and intense chocolate notes to dessert and martini-style cocktails.

before.name: Ouzo
before.description: Greek anise liqueur. Turns cloudy with water (the louche effect).

after.name: Ouzo
after.description: A traditional Greek anise-flavored aperitif, typically served with water which causes it to turn cloudy (the louche effect). Known for its strong, sweet licorice taste.

before.name: Sambuca
before.description: Italian anise liqueur. Often served as digestive shots with espresso.

after.name: Sambuca
after.description: An Italian anise-flavored liqueur, often served as a digestivo, sometimes with coffee beans ('con la mosca'). It has a sweet, potent licorice flavor and also louches with water.

before.name: Allspice Dram (Pimento)
before.description: Allspice-infused rum-based liqueur. Tiki workhorse.

after.name: Allspice Dram
after.description: A rum-based liqueur infused with allspice berries, also known as Pimento Dram. A crucial ingredient in many tiki cocktails, providing warm, complex baking spice notes.

before.name: Velvet Falernum
before.description: Lightly alcoholic falernum liqueur. Lime, ginger, almond, clove. Tiki essential.

after.name: Velvet Falernum
after.description: A low-alcohol liqueur from Barbados, flavored with lime, ginger, almond, and clove. An indispensable ingredient in tiki and tropical cocktails, adding a unique sweet and spicy complexity.

before.name: Angostura Aromatic Bitters
before.description: The world's most-used bitters. Spice and gentian; standard in Old Fashioneds and Manhattans.

after.name: Angostura Aromatic Bitters
after.description: The world's most iconic and widely used aromatic bitters, featuring a complex blend of herbs and spices. Essential for adding depth and balance to classic cocktails like the Old Fashioned and Manhattan.

before.name: Fee Brothers Whiskey Barrel-Aged Bitters
before.description: Aromatic bitters aged in whiskey barrels. Depth for Old Fashioneds.

after.name: Fee Brothers Whiskey Barrel-Aged Bitters
after.description: Aromatic bitters aged in whiskey barrels, imparting a smoky and oaky depth to cocktails. Ideal for enhancing whiskey-based drinks, adding complexity and a subtle barrel character.

before.name: Peychaud's Bitters
before.description: Anise-forward bitters. Required for Sazeracs.

after.name: Peychaud's Bitters
after.description: A classic aromatic bitters with a distinctive anise and cherry profile, originating from New Orleans. An indispensable ingredient for the Sazerac cocktail, providing its signature flavor and rosy hue.

before.name: Regan's Orange Bitters No. 6
before.description: Modern orange bitters. Common cocktail-bar default for orange bitters.

after.name: Regan's Orange Bitters No. 6
after.description: A modern classic orange bitters, developed by cocktail expert Gary Regan, known for its balanced blend of orange peel, cardamom, and other spices. A versatile and widely used choice for adding citrus and spice complexity to cocktails.

before.name: Fee Brothers Orange Bitters
before.description: Lower-proof orange bitters. Sweeter and brighter than Regan's.

after.name: Orange Bitters
after.description: Aromatic bitters with a prominent orange flavor, known for being sweeter and brighter. Used to enhance classic cocktails like the Old Fashioned and Manhattan.

before.name: Calvados
before.description: Generic French apple brandy. Aged in oak; Normandy region protected.

after.name: Calvados
after.description: An apple or pear brandy from the Normandy region of France, aged in oak barrels. Offers fruity and woody notes, often enjoyed neat or in cocktails like the Calvados Sour.

before.name: Porter
before.description: Generic porter. Dark and roasted but lighter than stout.

after.name: Porter
after.description: A dark, top-fermented beer known for its roasted malt flavors, often with notes of chocolate and coffee. Lighter in body than a stout, it's a versatile and approachable dark beer.

before.name: Bittermens Xocolatl Mole Bitters
before.description: Cacao and chili bitters. Adds depth to tequila and rum stirred drinks.

after.name: Xocolatl Mole Bitters
after.description: Complex bitters featuring cacao and chili, inspired by Mexican mole sauce. Adds depth and a spicy chocolate note to tequila, rum, and whiskey cocktails.

before.name: Bittermens Hellfire Habanero Shrub
before.description: Habanero pepper bitters. For spicy margaritas and modern bloody mary tweaks.

after.name: Hellfire Habanero Bitters
after.description: Intensely spicy bitters made with habanero peppers. Ideal for adding a fiery kick to margaritas, Bloody Marys, and other cocktails requiring heat.

before.name: Scrappy's Lavender Bitters
before.description: Lavender-forward bitters. Modern gin and floral cocktail use.

after.name: Lavender Bitters
after.description: Aromatic bitters with a distinct floral lavender profile. Perfect for adding a delicate, perfumed note to gin cocktails, sparkling wine, and modern creations.

before.name: Cachaça
before.description: Generic Brazilian cane spirit. Caipirinha base.

after.name: Cachaça
after.description: A Brazilian spirit distilled from fermented sugarcane juice. Known for its grassy and fruity notes, it is the essential base for the Caipirinha cocktail.

before.name: Other Rum
before.description: Catchall for Clairin, Batavia Arrack, Aguardiente.

after.name: Other Rum
after.description: A broad category for rum-like spirits that don't fit standard classifications, such as Clairin, Batavia Arrack, or Aguardiente. Offers diverse flavor profiles depending on origin and production.

before.name: Extra Anejo Tequila
before.description: Generic Extra Anejo tequila. Aged 3+ years; sipping category.

after.name: Extra Anejo Tequila
after.description: Tequila aged for a minimum of three years in oak barrels, resulting in a rich, complex spirit. Best enjoyed neat, offering deep notes of caramel, vanilla, and cooked agave.
